title: Band Space — Band Creation, Invites, Drive Folder, Members
problem: Band members need a shared space where all sessions are visible and access is controlled. Joining must be frictionless. The recorder's Google Drive folder is the audio source of truth — the app needs to bridge from 'here is an invite link' to 'you now have access to the band's recordings'.
analysis: Band metadata (name, members, Drive folder ID, invite code) stored in Postgres. Sessions and clips are stored in Postgres (not in Drive JSON files), so session/clip data is readable by all band members immediately after joining. Audio files live in the recorder's Drive folder. A user can belong to multiple bands. Band Settings page: member list with role management (pill buttons, not native select), invite link copy, storage quota bar. Remove member / Leave band both require confirmation via a bottom sheet before executing — prevents accidental destructive actions.
successCriteria:
- Create band: enter name → band record created in Postgres → invite link generated
- Invite link opens a join page: shows band name, prompts Google sign-in
- After joining, app shows member's Google email with clear instruction to recorder to share Drive folder
- Session screen shows QR code of current session URL
- Band member list: display name + email, role badge, join date
- Recorder can change member roles using styled pill buttons (Editor / Member) — no native select
- Remove member: visible button → confirmation sheet → delete from Postgres
- Leave band: visible button → confirmation sheet → redirect to /bands
- Multiple bands: user sees all their bands on the home screen
- Band roles: Recorder (full access + unfreeze), Editor (create versions + freeze + record), Member (vote + comment only)
- Default role for new members: Editor
implementation:
1. app/(auth)/bands/page.tsx: lists all bands for current user.
2. app/(auth)/bands/[bandId]/page.tsx: session list for a band.
3. app/api/bands/route.ts: POST creates a new band.
4. app/api/bands/[bandId]/members/route.ts: PATCH updates member role; DELETE removes member.
5. components/bands/BandSettingsClient.tsx: member list with inline pill buttons for role change (Editor/Member — active pill highlighted, tap other pill to switch), Remove and Leave band buttons both open a BottomSheet confirmation before executing, storage quota bar, invite link copy, "Manage files / sync" link to /bands/[bandId]/drive (Editor+ only).
6. app/(auth)/bands/[bandId]/settings/page.tsx: server component — loads band + membership, renders BandSettingsClient.
7. QR code for session URL rendered via react-qr-code in SessionHeaderClient.
8. app/(auth)/bands/[bandId]/drive/page.tsx: Drive file management server component — loads all clips with Drive file IDs across all sessions, redirects MEMBER role, renders DriveFilesClient.
9. components/bands/DriveFilesClient.tsx: Drive management UI — clips grouped by session with source/final status dots (grey=unchecked, green=ok, red=missing). Sync button checks all file IDs via /api/drive/check-file in batches of 5. Per-clip actions: Delete source (removes Drive file + clears driveFileId), Delete clip (removes entire clip record + both Drive files). Both actions require confirmation via BottomSheet with distinct destructive copy.
10. app/api/drive/check-file/route.ts: GET — checks if a Drive file exists (id,trashed fields). Verifies caller is a band member first.
11. app/api/clips/[clipId]/delete-source/route.ts: DELETE — removes source Drive file (best-effort), nulls driveFileId in DB. Editor+ only.

decisions.decision: Pill buttons for role change instead of native <select>
decisions.rationale: Native <select> is styled by the OS and looks visually inconsistent with the dark app design. Pill buttons (Editor | Member) are always visible, immediately tappable, and make the current role and the available roles obvious at a glance without opening a dropdown.
decisions.recommendation: Two pill buttons per member row (Editor, Member). Active role is highlighted with its badge color. Tapping the inactive pill calls changeRole() immediately.

decisions.decision: Remove/Leave actions require confirmation sheet before executing
decisions.rationale: Text underline links ('Remove', 'Leave band') have no visual weight — they look like navigation links, not destructive actions. A tap mis-aimed at the email text could accidentally trigger removal. A confirmation bottom sheet with a red danger button adds the right friction for an irreversible action.
decisions.recommendation: setRemoveConfirmEmail(userEmail) opens a BottomSheet. Sheet shows member name + consequence text. Confirm button calls removeMember(). Cancel closes sheet with no action.

decisions.decision: Postgres for band membership, Drive for audio access
decisions.rationale: Drive's drive.file scope prevents one user from reading files created by another. Band membership and session/clip metadata live in Postgres. Audio files live in the recorder's Drive, shared at the folder level via standard Drive sharing.
decisions.recommendation: Postgres tables: bands, band_members, sessions, clips, versions, votes, comments. Drive: audio files only.

decisions.decision: Manual Drive folder sharing — no programmatic share
decisions.rationale: Programmatic sharing requires storing and using the recorder's refresh token for Drive Permissions API calls — sensitive security surface. Manual sharing (recorder shares via Drive UI, one time per member) avoids this.
decisions.recommendation: After a member joins, show a clear prompt with their email address. Document this as a one-time step.

knownLimitations: New members cannot hear audio until the recorder manually shares the Drive folder with their Google email
If the recorder loses access to their Google account, band audio becomes inaccessible — no recovery path without exporting files first
Drive sync checks file existence but does not verify file integrity or playback quality
Deleting a clip from the Drive page removes it globally — all band members lose access immediately
userGuide: Create a band from the home screen. Share the invite link or QR code with bandmates — they sign in with Google and join as Editors. After someone joins, their email appears in Settings → Members. Share the Drive folder with that email from Google Drive (one-time step). In Settings, use the pill buttons to switch a member between Editor and Member role. Tap Remove to remove a member (confirmation required). Non-Recorder members can Leave band from their own member row (confirmation required). Editors and Recorders can open 'Manage files / sync' from the Storage section to see all Drive files grouped by session. Hit 'Sync' to check which files still exist in Drive (green dot = ok, red dot = missing). Delete a source file to free Drive space while keeping the clip and its versions. Delete a clip to remove it entirely from Odio and Drive.

statusHistory.reason: Role change: pill buttons replace native select. Remove/Leave: confirmation sheet replaces plain text links.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: BandSettingsClient: pill buttons for role change, BottomSheet confirmation for remove/leave actions

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Drive management page: DriveFilesClient with sync, delete-source, and delete-clip actions. check-file and delete-source API routes added.
